pages.raw_text:
Evans of the Master Plumbers asso-
citation said this afternoon.


"There is nothing we can give out
for publication at the present time.
Suffice to say that the plumbers are
still out. In a few days there will
likely be lots said from our view OF
the matter. They took us entirely by
surprise"


The meeting of the members of the
plumbers union lasted but very
short time The reports of the COM
mittees of the various shops were re-
ceived. Delegate Witherspoon refus-
ed to make any statement of the
plumbers side of the case today, say
ing that the union had instructed him
to say nothing.


The hours now enforced by the union
are eight to constitute day. On Satur-
days they only work half day.


There will likely be a meeting OF
the board of examiners as a sort of ar
bitration board. This it is believed is
favored by the master plumbers. This
board is composed OF James Smyth
and James Maxwell, plumbers; j. F.
Armstrong, journeyman plumber; j.
w. Legeson, plumbing inspector of the
city; and DR.. Roberts of the health
department, president of the board.


Only a part of the full membership
Of the union was present when the ac-
ton was taken to strike.


The proposition was not brought be-
fore the trades council for their en-
